explanation: This LTL property states that if there is an information request, then the robot must provide a reason for its path selection. Specifically, at any point in time, if an infoRequest occurs, it must eventually provide a reason (provideReason) and if developer is true it must provide an explanation of the intended path (explainIntendedPath). The property guarantees that the explanation will be provided in the future.

explanation:
This LTL formula ensures that if information is requested (`infoRequest`), then the robot will eventually provide a detailed explanation (`detailRequested`) as to why it chose a particular route. This is achieved by the use of a template G((! (bool_exp1 & (X (! bool_exp1)))) | (X (((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)) V (bool_exp2 -> ((G[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3) | (((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)) V bool_exp3))))))) & ((! bool_exp1) -> (((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)) V (bool_exp2 -> ((G[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3) | (((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)) V bool_exp3)))))) with the following components. 

`bool_exp1` is defined as `infoRequest` is a condition that is expected to start, then, only when infoRequest happens, it is also expected that the detailed explanation will start eventually (`detailRequested`) of why it has chosen the route. `bool_exp2` is set to `provideReason`, implying the robot will provide a reason. `bool_exp3`  is defined as `explainCurrentPath`. The formula utilizes the `G` (Globally) operator. It also makes sure to start only when `infoRequest` takes place, this is made possible by (! bool_exp1). Finally `N_DURATION` is set to 5 to allow the formula to be checked.

(bool_exp1 & (X (! bool_exp1)))) | (X (((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)) V (bool_exp2 -> ((G[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3) | (((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)) V bool_exp3))))))) & ((! bool_exp1) -> (((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)) V (bool_exp2 -> ((G[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3) | (((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)) V bool_exp3))))))
